item.title: Team Secretary (Rgn) in PAYMENTS, SETTLEMENTS AND INNOVATION SUPERVISION HOD OFFICE
item.description: Leeds only based role. Directorate Overview FMID sits at the heart of the Bank and is responsible for supervision, policy and risk analysis of key financial market infrastructures (e.g. payment systems, CCPs and securities depositories). The directorate works closely across the Bank, with external firms, and with international regulators in a highly collaborative environment. Job Description We are seeking a motivated secretary to provide support to Senior Managers and their teams in the Payment Settlement and Innovation Supervision Divisions. This would suit an enterprising secretary who is looking to develop their skills and gain a wide experience in a busy office. The jobholder would form part of a wider team supporting the directorate. This is an exciting position with a varied and interesting workload with opportunities to display your own initiative. It will suit someone who is able to prioritise their workload and co‑ordinate numerous tasks, as the role comes with a wide range of responsibilities. You need to be well-organised; a good communicator; confident at collaborating closely at all levels (internally and externally); be flexible; and able to work successfully within the team. The Senior Managers have busy diaries which require effective management, arranging internal and external meetings. Duties include: arranging domestic and international travel; processing expenses; coordinating Joiner/Mover/Leaver processes within the teams; and act as a point of contact for the teams. The support team is self-covering so the jobholder will be required to help cover absences on the unit, including covering the Head of Division Personal Assistant role. Role Requirements Provide support to Senior Managers and their teams Manage diaries, including scheduling, prioritisation and resolving clashes Coordinate supervisory meetings with external firms (including papers and agendas) Act as a key liaison point for external firms, ensuring professional and effective engagement Support governance processes where needed Arrange travel and process expenses Liaise with colleagues across FMID, wider Bank teams, and external firms Provide cover and support across the wider admin team Minimum Criteria Team Secretary or administrative experience Diary management experience Microsoft Office skills Essential Criteria Strong organisational skills High level of accuracy and attention to detail Ability to prioritise multiple tasks and issues quickly and efficiently Ability to work well in a supportive team environment Strong social skills and collaboration Proactive and adaptable approach Willingness to adopt new technology and improve processes Desirable Criteria Experience of having covered at HoD PA level. You should not be put off applying if you do not meet all of these criteria – we would encourage you to get in touch to discuss what support you might need to close any gaps and/or any other skills you might have which you think could be helpful in role. Our Approach to Inclusion The Bank values diversity, equity and inclusion. We play a key role in maintaining monetary and financial stability, and to do that effectively, we believe we need a workforce that reflects the society we serve. At the Bank of England, we want all colleagues to feel valued and respected, so we're working hard to build an inclusive culture which supports people from all backgrounds and communities to be at their best at work. We celebrate all forms of diversity, including (but not limited to) age, disability, ethnicity, gender, gender identity, race, religion, sexual orientation and socioeconomic status. We believe that it’s by drawing on different perspectives and experiences that we’ll continue to make the best decisions for the public. We welcome applications from individuals who work flexibly, including job shares and part time working patterns. We've also partnered with external organisations to support us in making adjustments for candidates and employees in the recruitment process where they're needed. For most roles where work can be carried out at home, we aim for colleagues to spend half of their time in the office, with a minimum of 40% per month. Subject to that minimum requirement, individuals and managers should work together to find what works best for them, their team and stakeholders. Finally, we're proud to be a member of the Disability Confident Scheme . If you wish to apply under this scheme, you should check the box in the ‘Candidate Personal Information’ under the ‘Disability Confident Scheme’ section of the application. Salary and Benefits Information We encourage flexible working, part time working and job share arrangements. Part time salary and benefits will be on a pro-rated basis as appropriate. In addition, we also offer a comprehensive benefits package as detailed below: Currently a non-contributory, career average pension giving you a guaranteed retirement benefit of 1/80th of your annual salary for every year worked. There is the option to increase your pension (to 1/65th) or decrease (to 1/105th) in exchange for salary through our flexible benefits programme each year. The Bank has the discretion to vary standard accrual rates and dial up and dial down rates at any time and to withdraw dial up and dial down options at any time. A discretionary performance award based on a current award pool. An 8% benefits allowance with the option to take as salary or purchase a wide range of flexible benefits. 26 days’ annual leave with option to buy up to 12 additional days through flexible benefits. Private medical insurance and income protection. National Security Vetting Process Employment in this role will be subject to the National Security Vetting clearance process (and typically can take between 6 to 12 weeks post offer) and the passing of additional Bank security checks in accordance with the Bank policy. Further information regarding the vetting and security clearance requirements for the role will be provided to the successful applicant, and information about how the Bank processes personal data for these purposes, is set out in the Bank's Privacy Notice . The Bank of England welcomes applications from all candidates, but as a UK Visas and Immigration (UKVI) approved sponsor, we have a responsibility to comply with the Immigration Rules and guidance. As such, our ability to employ individuals who require sponsorship for immigration purposes is limited. The Bank cannot guarantee that you and / or the role you are applying for will be eligible for sponsorship and that any application made to UKVI will be successful. Eligibility will therefore be considered on a case by case basis. The Application Process Important: Please ensure that you complete the ‘work history’ section and answer ALL the application questions fully. All candidate applications are anonymised to ensure that our hiring managers will not be able to see your personal information, including your CV, when reviewing your application details at the screening stage. It’s therefore really important that you fill out the work history and application form questions, as your answers will form a critical part of the initial selection process. The assessment process will comprise of two interview stages. (Amend and add brief details if relevant, such as interview/assessment dates or process for your specific role) This role closes on 12 August 2026. Please apply online, ensuring that you complete your work history and answer ALL the application questions fully and in detail as your application will not be considered if all mandatory questions are not fully completed.
item.dc:creator: Bank of England

item.title: Events Organiser in Events and Services
item.description: Location: London Directorate Overview Central Operations creates and maintains safe, sustainable and collaborative environments across all Bank locations, enabling and protecting the Bank in delivering its mission. The Directorate provides a range of operational services that support business continuity, enhance colleague and stakeholder experience, and ensure the effective running of the Bank's facilities and corporate activities. Job Description The Events Organiser supports the planning, coordination and delivery of internal and external events across the Bank. Working closely with colleagues, business areas and senior stakeholders, you will help ensure events are delivered professionally, efficiently and to a high standard. The role involves coordinating a variety of event activities, including delegate management, venue logistics, catering, audio-visual requirements and stakeholder communications. You will play a key role in ensuring events run smoothly and provide a positive experience for attendees. You will work collaboratively with a range of internal teams and suppliers, developing your event management skills while contributing to the successful delivery of seminars, conferences, meetings and stakeholder engagement events. Key Responsibilities Support the planning and delivery of internal and external events. Liaise with stakeholders to understand event requirements and provide administrative and logistical support. Manage delegate registration processes and maintain accurate event records using event management systems. Coordinate event bookings, catering, AV requirements, room layouts and event materials. Prepare event documentation, attendee communications and briefing materials. Provide on-the-day event support, resolving minor issues and escalating where appropriate. Work closely with internal service providers and suppliers to ensure delivery requirements are met. Monitor event timelines and ensure key milestones and actions are completed. Gather attendee feedback and support post-event reviews. Contribute ideas for improving event processes and customer experience. Ensure compliance with relevant Bank policies, procedures and health and safety requirements. Minimum Criteria Demonstrable organisational skills, with evidence of managing tasks accurately and meeting deadlines. Proven communication skills, with examples of clear written and verbal communication. Experience using Microsoft Office applications, including Outlook, Word, Excel, PowerPoint and Teams. Essential Criteria Demonstrable experience managing multiple tasks and prioritising competing deadlines. Experience delivering a customer-focused service with a proactive and professional approach. Proven ability to work independently and collaboratively as part of a team. Strong problem-solving skills, with the ability to remain calm under pressure. Evidence of building effective working relationships with colleagues and stakeholder Flexibility to support event delivery, including occasional overnight stays. Desirable Criteria Experience organising or supporting events, meetings or conferences. Experience of customer service, hospitality, administration or operational support. Knowledge of Microsoft Teams, Teams Townhall or virtual event platforms. Experience using booking, registration or event management systems. Ability to identify opportunities to improve processes and ways of working. Our Approach to Inclusion The Bank values diversity, equity and inclusion. We play a key role in maintaining monetary and financial stability, and to do that effectively, we believe we need a workforce that reflects the society we serve. At the Bank of England, we want all colleagues to feel valued and respected, so we're working hard to build an inclusive culture which supports people from all backgrounds and communities to be at their best at work. We celebrate all forms of diversity, including (but not limited to) age, disability, ethnicity, gender, gender identity, race, religion, sexual orientation and socioeconomic status. We believe that it’s by drawing on different perspectives and experiences that we’ll continue to make the best decisions for the public. We welcome applications from individuals who work flexibly, including job shares and part time working patterns. We've also partnered with external organisations to support us in making adjustments for candidates and employees in the recruitment process where they're needed. For most roles where work can be carried out at home, we aim for colleagues to spend half of their time in the office, individuals and managers should work together to find what works best for them, their team and stakeholders. Finally, we're proud to be a member of the Disability Confident Scheme . If you wish to apply under this scheme, you should check the box in the ‘Candidate Personal Information’ under the ‘Disability Confident Scheme’ section of the application. Salary and Benefits Information This position offers an annual salary of £28,640. In addition, we also offer a comprehensive benefits package as detailed below: Currently a non-contributory, career average pension giving you a guaranteed retirement benefit of 1/80th of your annual salary for every year worked. There is the option to increase your pension (to 1/65th) or decrease (to 1/105th) in exchange for salary through our flexible benefits programme each year. The Bank has the discretion to vary standard accrual rates and dial up and dial down rates at any time and to withdraw dial up and dial down options at any time. A discretionary performance award based on a current award pool. An 8% benefits allowance with the option to take as salary or purchase a wide range of flexible benefits. 26 days’ annual leave with option to buy up to 12 additional days through flexible benefits. Private medical insurance and income protection. National Security Vetting Process Employment in this role will be subject to the National Security Vetting clearance process (and typically can take between 6 to 12 weeks post offer) and the passing of additional Bank security checks in accordance with the Bank policy. Further information regarding the vetting and security clearance requirements for the role will be provided to the successful applicant, and information about how the Bank processes personal data for these purposes, is set out in the Bank's Privacy Notice . The Bank of England welcomes applications from all candidates, but as a UK Visas and Immigration (UKVI) approved sponsor, we have a responsibility to comply with the Immigration Rules and guidance. As such, our ability to employ individuals who require sponsorship for immigration purposes is limited. The Bank cannot guarantee that you and / or the role you are applying for will be eligible for sponsorship and that any application made to UKVI will be successful. Eligibility will therefore be considered on a case by case basis. The Application Process Important: Please ensure that you complete the ‘work history’ section and answer ALL the application questions fully. All candidate applications are anonymised to ensure that our hiring managers will not be able to see your personal information, including your CV, when reviewing your application details at the screening stage. It’s therefore really important that you fill out the work history and application form questions, as your answers will form a critical part of the initial selection process. The assessment process will comprise of two stages. This role closes for applications on 5 August 2026. Please apply online, ensuring that you complete your work history and answer ALL the application questions fully and in detail as your application will not be considered if all mandatory questions are not fully completed.
item.dc:creator: Bank of England

item.title: Corporate Governance Team Manager
item.description: Corporate Governance Team Manager Full time / Part time Salary: £44,297- £ 49,813, with potential for further progression to £56,082 with our pay progression scheme. Location: Hybrid Contracted to our Manchester, London, Edinburgh, Cardiff or Belfast office. Through hybrid working we offer flexible home and office-based working opportunities and there will be times when you will be expected to attend the office to collaborate with colleagues or travel due to business need. Why work for the ICO? Pay progression scheme. Hybrid and flexible working options. 25 days paid holiday per year, plus privilege and public holidays. Flexi leave (up to 26 additional days leave per year). Pension (employer contribution around 28.9%). Online discount scheme to save money at major supermarkets, retailers, gyms, restaurants, insurance providers and many more. Health Cash Plan. Fantastic development opportunities to learn and progress. Further details can be found on the benefits section of our website . Job summary As Corporate Governance Team Manager, you will lead the development and continuous improvement of the ICO’s corporate governance framework, ensuring that governance arrangements remain robust, compliant and aligned with strategic priorities. Reporting to the Corporate Governance and Secretariat Group Manager, you will act as a trusted adviser to senior leaders, providing expert guidance on governance matters and supporting high-profile projects that shape how the organisation operates. The Information Commissioner’s Office (ICO) is the independent regulator of information rights. In a data-driven world, we provide advice, guidance, and support to organisations enabling compliance with their obligations, as well as protecting individuals and their personal data. As an employer, we are passionate about making a positive difference to the lives and careers of our people, and we empower you to be curious, impactful, collaborative and respectful. Job description You will act as a subject matter expert, providing high-level advice and guidance on governance matters, supporting governance-related projects, and contributing to the organisation’s strategic governance agenda. The role supports the integration of governance processes across the organisation and ensures compliance with internal protocols and external standards. In addition, you will provide day-to-day leadership and management to a Governance & Secretariat Support Officer, supporting their development and ensuring the effective delivery of governance and secretariat services. This role requires strong analytical and communication skills, professional judgement, and the ability to influence and collaborate with senior stakeholders. It contributes directly to the effectiveness and credibility of the ICO’s governance framework, while helping to develop capability and resilience within the wider Governance and Secretariat function. Key responsibilities: Governance Frameworks & Processes- Lead the development and continuous improvement of corporate governance arrangements, ensuring frameworks, policies, and procedures are strategically aligned, operationally effective, and responsive to organisational and external requirements. Design and embed governance mechanisms that enable sound decision-making, clear accountability, and regulatory compliance. Strategic Governance Projects- Lead and contribute to governance-related projects and cross-functional initiatives, applying specialist knowledge to deliver improvements and support strategic objectives. Advice & Best Practice- Act as a subject matter expert, providing authoritative advice and guidance on governance matters to senior managers and operational teams. Monitor developments in governance best practice and translate them into actionable improvements. Reporting & Communications- Produce high-quality governance reports, briefings, and communications for internal and external stakeholders, including senior leadership and oversight bodies. Lead the delivery of the Annual Report, ensuring governance disclosures are accurate, comprehensive, and aligned with statutory and best practice requirements. Accountability & Delegation Frameworks- Work collaboratively with colleagues in People Services and senior managers to maintain accountability frameworks, ensuring it supports clear lines of responsibility and effective governance. Lead the review and update of the Scheme of Delegations, ensuring it remains current, well-structured, and aligned with organisational decision-making and accountability arrangements. Stakeholder Engagement - Build and maintain effective relationships with internal stakeholders and external partners, promoting governance awareness and consistency in application. Leadership & Oversight- Manage the Corporate Governance Officer role, providing effective leadership and oversight. Act as deputy to the Corporate Governance & Secretariat Group Manager during periods of absence, contributing to continuity and resilience within the function. Decision-Making & Problem Solving- Make operational decisions within a defined governance framework, using sound judgement and analytical skills. Refer novel or complex issues to senior specialists or legal advisors as appropriate. Person specification Essential criteria assessed at application stage: Relevant experience in corporate governance, risk, or compliance within a public sector or regulated environment. Demonstrable operational knowledge of governance frameworks, processes, and regulatory requirements. Experience leading governance projects and contributing to strategic initiatives. Proven ability to provide expert advice and guidance on governance matters to senior stakeholders. Essential criteria assessed during interview: Strong understanding of corporate governance principles, statutory reporting requirements, and governance best practice. Ability to interpret, apply and advise on governance frameworks, policies and procedures within complex organisational environments. Excellent analytical, problem-solving and decision-making skills, with sound judgement in managing complex or sensitive issues. Highly effective written and verbal communication skills, including the ability to produce clear reports, briefings and recommendations for senior audiences. Strong stakeholder engagement and relationship management skills, with the ability to build credibility across internal and external networks. Experience of influencing and providing professional advice to senior leaders to support governance-related decision-making. Ability to work independently, show initiative and deliver outcomes within established frameworks and timescales. Collaborative and adaptable approach, with a commitment to continuous improvement and high-quality service delivery. Excellent organisational skills, attention to detail, and the ability to manage competing priorities effectively. Demonstrable integrity, discretion and professionalism, including experience of supporting, developing or mentoring others in governance practice. Equality, diversity, and inclusion The ICO is committed to promoting and enhancing equality, diversity, and inclusion. We are focused on developing a workforce that is representative of the communities we serve and together we are building an inclusive workplace where all of our colleagues have the opportunity to make a real difference. We are championing this through our Equality Diversity and Inclusion Board together with a number of staff networks. Read more about our commitment on our website. Candidates with a disability who meet the minimum criteria for this vacancy will be invited to interview as part of the ICO’s commitment to the Disability Confident Scheme. As part of the ICO’s commitment to our EDI objectives and creating a workplace that represents the communities and societies we serve, we guarantee an interview to candidates who declare they identify as belonging from an ethnic minority background and who meet the minimum criteria for this vacancy If you are disabled or have an impairment and require an alternative application method, please email the HR team at recruitment@ico.org.uk Closing Date Please submit your CV and cover letter by 23:59, 16 August . Your cover letter should be no more than 1,000 words and should clearly demonstrate how you meet the essential criteria for the role. We may close this vacancy early if we receive a high volume of applications. To ensure your application is considered, we encourage you to apply as soon as possible. If you require any reasonable adjustments to support your application, please contact us at recruitment@ico.org.uk . In the event of a high volume of applications, we may not be able to invite all candidates who meet the minimum criteria to interview. However, we encourage you stay in touch and apply for future roles that match your interests. All candidates who meet the minimum criteria and apply in-line with our guaranteed interview scheme for disabled and ethnic minority applicants will be interviewed. Clearance Required for this role: Baseline Personnel Security Standard (BPSS)
item.dc:creator: Information Commissioner's Office

item.title: Lead Solution Architect
item.description: Lead Solutions Architect Full time/Part Time Salary: £57,815 - £65,021 pa, with potential for further progression to £73,211 pa with our pay progression scheme. ​ Location: Hybrid Contracted to our Wilmslow, London, Edinburgh, Cardiff or Belfast office, we will be relocating to Manchester city centre from 28 September 2026. Once the move takes place your contracted head office will be Circle Square, Manchester. Through hybrid working we offer flexible home and office-based working opportunities and there will be times when you will be expected to attend the office to collaborate with colleagues or travel due to business need. Why work for the ICO? Pay progression scheme. Hybrid and flexible working options. 25 days paid holiday per year, plus privilege and public holidays. Flexi leave (up to 24 additional days leave per year). Pension (employer contribution around 28.9%). Online discount scheme to save money at major supermarkets, retailers, gyms, restaurants, insurance providers and many more. Health Cash Plan. Fantastic development opportunities to learn and progress. Further details can be found on the benefits section of our website . About us The Information Commissioner’s Office (ICO) is the independent regulator of information rights. In a data-driven world, we provide advice, guidance, and support to organisations enabling compliance with their obligations, as well as protecting individuals and their personal data. As an employer, we are passionate about making a positive difference to the lives and careers of our people, and we empower you to be curious, impactful, collaborative and respectful. Job Summary This role will lead the design and implementation of comprehensive solution designs that align with the organisation’s strategic objectives and Enterprise Architecture principles. The role will help the Director of Architecture to ensure the quality of solution designs and deployments across the organisation, ensuring the performance, security and scalability of those designs. Job Description The role will provide technical guidance and direction throughout the solution development lifecycle, including requirements validation technology evaluation, design and benefits realisation tracking The successful candidate will collaborate and consult with a wide variety of stakeholders to ensure solution design decisions are aligned with Enterprise Architecture strategy They will also help the Director of Architecture create an Enterprise Architecture roadmap to assist in future planning of the solution needs of the ICO as well as help the Director of Architecture to ensure consistency of solution designs across the ICO and facilitate the identification and integration of new technologies to create resilient and flexible solution designs that supports current business objectives and future growth. The role will carry out horizon scanning across the industry and wider technological landscape, identifying innovative tools and trends that could enhance the capabilities of the ICO Key Responsibilities Developing and delivering the ICO’s Solution Architecture vision, strategy and roadmaps Lead the design of scalable and efficient solutions that meet business needs, justifying and communicating design decisions Collaborate closely with stakeholders and product managers to gather and analyse requirements for new solutions Create a library of architecture and solution building blocks to direct and guide effective and robust solution design Creating and managing the solution design and development of services of various sizes, including how they interact with their surroundings and how they evolve over time Supporting technical specialists in multiple projects and services on problems that require broad architectural thinking Defining how we build user-centered systems in an agile environment to serve a variety of citizen and business needs To challenge existing practices, looking for deeper problems to solve and larger opportunities for digital transformation Communicating the architecture vision to technical and non-technical stakeholders and to work in multi-disciplinary teams Playing an active role in the ICO Architecture community and related communities of practice where you will share your knowledge of best practice, tools and techniques Helping to build a diverse, inclusive culture across the architecture community, growing awareness, inclusivity and balance Working with other architects, colleagues and peers to define standards and guidance around best practice Engaging and networking with people across the ICO up to Director level to understand their needs and ensure they are aligned with the Enterprise Architecture vision To lead the implementation of cloud-based solutions using Azure services such as virtual machines, storage, networking, and security To participate in the development of best practices and standards for Azure solution development and implementation. To be an evangelist for Azure solution architectures and best practices Essential criteria assessed at application stage Substantial experience relevant to the role requirements, as described in the role responsibilities and person specification, and accumulated through any combination of academic or vocational qualifications or experience. Experience of working with cloud-first architectures and how to design solutions that take advantage of the opportunities they offer. Experience of working with technical teams and delivering user-centered services in an agile environment. Experience of working with stakeholders of all levels to make decisions on technology choices, using the most appropriate languages, frameworks and tools to meet user and business needs. Experience of working with Enterprise Architecture frameworks (TOGAF, Zachman) Proven ability to design and document end-to-end solutions, including consideration of data and infrastructure components. Knowledge of a wide range of technologies including cloud platforms (Microsoft Azure), microservices, APIs and enterprise software. Essential criteria assessed at interview stage Excellent analytical skills to diagnose issues, evaluate risk and develop viable solutions Knowledge of a wide range of technologies including cloud platforms (Microsoft Azure), microservices, APIs and enterprise software. Proficiency in creating solution architecture design diagrams, roadmaps and associated artefacts. Ability to deal with complexity and ambiguity, creative problem solving and developing innovative solutions. Excellent communication skills for educating employees on solution architecture. Able to communicate complex insights and principles simply and with impact, particularly across technical and non-technical audiences. Competent at negotiating and persuading where necessary, whilst applying a highly ethical approach. Commercial acumen, understanding how solution architecture links to business value creation Equality, diversity and inclusion The ICO is committed to promoting and enhancing equality, diversity, and inclusion. We are focused on developing a workforce that is representative of the communities we serve and together we are building an inclusive workplace where all of our colleagues have the opportunity to make a real difference. We are championing this through our Equality Diversity and Inclusion Board together with a number of staff networks. Read more about our commitment on our website. Candidates with a disability who meet the minimum criteria for this vacancy will be invited to interview as part of the ICO’s commitment to the Disability Confident Scheme. As part of the ICO’s commitment to our EDI objectives and creating a workplace that represents the communities and societies we serve, we guarantee an interview to candidates who declare they identify as belonging from an ethnic minority background and who meet the minimum criteria for this vacancy If you are disabled or have an impairment and require an alternative application method, please email the HR team at recruitment@ico.org.uk Closing Date Please submit your CV by 23:59, Sunday 16th August 2026 We reserve the right to close this vacancy before this date should we receive sufficient applications. Please apply as soon as possible to ensure your application is considered. We may close this vacancy early if we receive a high volume of applications. To ensure your application is considered, we encourage you to apply as soon as possible. If you require any reasonable adjustments to support your application, please contact us at recruitment@ico.org.uk. In the event of a high volume of applications, we may not be able to invite all candidates who meet the minimum criteria to interview. However, we encourage you stay in touch and apply for future roles that match your interests. All candidates who meet the minimum criteria and apply in-line with our guaranteed interview scheme for disabled and ethnic minority applicants will be interviewed. Clearance Required for this role: Baseline Personnel Security Standard (BPSS)

item.title: Senior Global Strategy & Engagement Advisor - US & The Americas
item.description: Job title: Senior Global Strategy & Engagement Advisor - US & The Americas Division: Supervision, Policy and Competition Department: International Division – Global Strategy and Engagement Team Salary: National (Edinburgh and Leeds) ranging from £53,800 to £70,000 and London £59,200 to £75,000 per annum (Salary offered will be based on skills and experience) This role is graded as: Senior Associate, Regulatory Your external recruitment contact is Shafika via Shafika.shaikh@fca.org.uk Your internal recruitment contact is Iona Magee via iona.magee@fca.org.uk. Applications must be submitted through our online portal. Applications sent via social media or email will not be accepted. About the FCA and team We regulate financial services firms in the UK, to keep financial markets fair, thriving and effective. By joining us, you’ll play a key part in protecting consumers, driving economic growth, and shaping the future of UK finance services. SPC oversees firms and individuals (supervision), creates and reviews the rules by which they operate (policy) and identifies and remedies ineffective competition in markets (competition). The International Division coordinates our strategy for international engagement, working collaboratively across the FCA to ensure the organization delivers on its international priorities. Role responsibilities Helping shape and prioritise International Division’s strategic engagement and approach with authorities in the United States, Canada and the wider Americas Maintaining key relationships with counterparts in the USA and the Americas, government bodies and secondees Developing, delivering and supporting creative strategy and engagement plans on priority topics, working closely with colleagues across the FCA to ensure a coordinated and considered approach Advising and supporting FCA Executive Directors and the Senior Leadership Team in their engagements with key international partners, including in relation to overseas visits Analysing key policy and political developments across jurisdictions, shaping global policy and providing advice on the opportunities and challenges arising for the FCA Advise on risks and opportunities for the FCA Contribute to coordinated international strategy Operate effectively in a changing policy environment Skills required Minimum: Prior experience of the international financial regulatory agenda gained through practical experience / experience working on regional policy issues Prior experience of advising and working with internal and external stakeholders Prior analytical experience of evaluating complex information quickly, making sound judgements supported by evidence Essential: Ability to identify, manage and support stakeholders, building rapport and constructive relationships Effective written and oral communication skills, including the ability to explain complex issues succinctly and compellingly, adapted to the audience Motivated approach and ability for problem-solving Inclusive and collaborative working method, with ability to draw on a range of knowledge across the wider organisation to deliver Well-developed self-management and organisational skills, delivering multiple tasks while meeting deadlines Clear understanding of the FCA’s functions and objectives within the UK financial regulatory framework Benefits 25 days annual leave plus bank holidays Non-contributory pension (8–12% depending on age) and life assurance at eight times your salary Private healthcare with Bupa, income protection, and 24/7 Employee Assistance 35 hours of paid volunteering annually Hybrid model where employees work a minimum of 40% in the office each month (expectation of 50% for senior leaders). Changing from September to a minimum of 50% in the office each month (expectation of 60% for Directors and Executive Directors) A flexible benefits scheme designed around your lifestyle For a full list of our benefits, and our recruitment process as a whole visit our benefits page . Our values and culture Our colleagues are the key to our success as a regulator. We are committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive culture: one that’s free from discrimination and bias, celebrates difference, and supports colleagues to deliver at their best. We believe that our differences and similarities enable us to be a better organisation – one that makes better decisions, drives innovation, and delivers better regulation. If you require any adjustments due to a disability or condition, your recruiter is here to help - reach out for tailored support. We welcome diverse working styles and aim to find flexible solutions that suit both the role and individual needs, including options like part-time and job sharing where applicable. Disability confident: our hiring approach We’re proud to be a Disability Confident Employer , and therefore, people or individuals with disabilities and long-term conditions who best meet the minimum criteria for a role will go through to the next stage of the recruitment process. In cases of high application volumes we may progress applicants whose experience most closely matches the role’s key requirements. Useful information and timelines Advert Close Date: 5th August 2026 CV Review/Shortlist: 7th August 2026 First Interview: W/C 17th August 2026 Your Recruiter will discuss the process in detail with you during screening for the role, therefore, please make them aware if you are going to be unavailable for any date during this time.

item.title: Senior Developer
item.description: Senior Developer Division: Data Technology and Innovation Department : Digital Delivery Hub Salary: Leeds ranging from £65,000 to £80,000 This role is graded as: Lead Associate - Regulatory Office Location: Leeds only Your external recruitment contact is Benjamin via benjamin.paulon@fca.org.uk Your internal recruitment contact is Pat via Patrycja.Matachowska@fca.org.uk Applications must be submitted through our online portal. Applications sent via social media or email will not be accepted. About the FCA and team We regulate financial services firms in the UK, to keep financial markets fair, thriving and effective. By joining us, you’ll play a key part in protecting consumers, driving economic growth, and shaping the future of UK finance services. The Data, Technology and Innovation (DTI) division enables the FCA to be a digital-first, data-led smart regulator by delivering a secure, agile, and cost-effective technology and data ecosystem that drives better decisions, transparency, and operational efficiency. Sitting within DTI, the Digital Delivery Hub digitally transforms the FCA's regulatory services to efficiently meet the needs of all stakeholders. Role responsibilities Lead the delivery of high-quality digital solutions by guiding development activity, mentoring colleagues and strengthening team capability to achieve successful outcomes Take ownership of technical incidents, support issues and defect resolution to ensure reliable services and minimise disruption for users Deliver development artefacts aligned to product requirements, enabling the creation of scalable and effective digital products that meet business needs Champion engineering best practice by leading initiatives that enhance ways of working and contribute to a growing community of practice across the organisation Influence wider engineering strategy by sharing technical insight and identifying opportunities to improve development standards and delivery capability Foster a culture of continuous improvement by promoting agile ways of working, collaboration and ongoing professional development across the team Play a key role in shaping the future of digital delivery, contributing to high-profile projects that support organisational objectives and improve outcomes for consumers Build technical and regulatory expertise while benefiting from flexible working, a supportive environment and an inclusive culture that values diverse perspectives and innovation Skills required Minimum: Experience as a Senior Developer including a solid understanding of software engineering principles and their application. Experience of Cloud Native development; ideally on AWS High level of experience in either Java/C# and React/Angular Essential: Degree-level qualification in Computing, Computer Science or a related discipline, or equivalent practical experience gained within software engineering environments Strong understanding of development automation, using tools and practices that improve efficiency, quality and software delivery outcomes Effective communication skills with the ability to engage confidently within multidisciplinary teams and with a broad range of stakeholders Proven ability to collaborate with engineers, designers, product managers and analysts to deliver high-quality solutions aligned to user and business needs Commitment to software engineering best practice, producing high-quality code that is coherent, loosely coupled, maintainable, well-documented and fully testable Ability to evaluate, select and apply innovative development approaches, adapting practices and frameworks to meet technical and delivery requirements Experience working within agile environments, alongside Solution and Technical Architects, with an understanding of system architecture, implementation trade-offs and Continuous Integration and Continuous Delivery practices Benefits 25 days annual leave plus bank holidays Hybrid model where employees work a minimum of 40% in the office each month (expectation of 50% for senior leaders). Changing from September to a minimum of 50% in the office each month (expectation of 60% for Directors and Executive Directors) Non-contributory pension (8–12% depending on age) and life assurance at eight times your salary Private healthcare with Bupa, income protection, and 24/7 Employee Assistance 35 hours of paid volunteering annually A flexible benefits scheme designed around your lifestyle For a full list of our benefits, and our recruitment process as a whole visit our benefits page. Our values & culture Our colleagues are the key to our success as a regulator. We are committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive culture: one that’s free from discrimination and bias, celebrates difference, and supports colleagues to deliver at their best. We believe that our differences and similarities enable us to be a better organisation – one that makes better decisions, drives innovation, and delivers better regulation. If you require any adjustments due to a disability or condition, your recruiter is here to help - reach out for tailored support. We welcome diverse working styles and aim to find flexible solutions that suit both the role and individual needs, including options like part-time and job sharing where applicable. Disability Confident: our hiring approach We’re proud to be a Disability Confident Employer, and therefore, people or individuals with disabilities and long-term conditions who best meet the minimum criteria for a role will go through to the next stage of the recruitment process. In cases of high application volumes, we may progress applicants whose experience most closely matches the role’s key requirements. Useful information and timeline Advert closing: 29th July at 11:59pm CV shortlist: 31st July Interviews: from 6th August This position is based in our Leeds office and applicants must be able to work from this location Your Recruiter will discuss the process in detail with you during screening for the role, therefore, please make them aware if you are going to be unavailable for any date during this time.

item.title: Manager Consumer Contracts Team
item.description: Job Title: Manager Consumer Contracts Team Division: Supervision, Policy & Competition (SPC) Department: Cross-Cutting Response, Research & Contracts Salary: National (Edinburgh and Leeds) ranging from £74,900 to £103,033 and London £82,300 to £113,300 (salary offered will be based on skills and experience) This role is graded as: Manager – Regulatory Your external recruitment contact is Steve Christopher via steve.christopher@fca.org.uk . Your internal recruitment contact is Katherine Whipps via katherine.whipps@fca.org.uk . Applications must be submitted through our online portal. Applications sent via social media or email will not be accepted. About the FCA and team We regulate financial services firms in the UK, to keep financial markets fair, thriving and effective. By joining us, you’ll play a key part in protecting consumers, driving economic growth and shaping the future of financial services in the UK. SPC oversees regulated firms and individuals (Supervision), creates and reviews the rules by which they operate (Policy) and identifies and remedies ineffective competition in markets (Competition). We are seeking an experienced and collaborative leader to manage the Consumer Contracts Team (CCT), a specialist team focused on protecting consumers by ensuring firms use fair, transparent and accessible contract terms and treat customers fairly throughout their contractual relationships. As Manager of the CCT, you will lead a team of specialists responsible for addressing unfair or unclear contract terms through a combination of legal expertise, regulatory insight and stakeholder engagement. You will provide direction across the team’s casework, projects and policy activities, working with internal and external stakeholders to deliver our statutory objectives and support key FCA priorities, including Consumer Duty and the review of Claims Management practices. Role responsibilities Manage, support and develop a team of contract law specialists, creating an inclusive environment where people can thrive, collaborate and deliver shared success Shape a positive team culture that promotes wellbeing, continuous learning and high-quality outcomes for consumers Oversee complex referrals and casework, helping secure fair treatment and redress for consumers where appropriate Carry out proactive projects that identify and reduce risks arising from unfair or unclear contract terms, improving outcomes across financial services Lead policy development and maintain regulatory guidance under the Consumer Rights Act 2015 and Unfair Terms in Consumer Contracts Regulations 1999, helping to shape standards across the sector Build good relationships with internal and external stakeholders to support consumer protection, help guide FCA priorities and help improve outcomes for millions of consumers across the UK Skills required Minimum: Prior experience working in a regulatory, enforcement, legal or compliance environment, with a clear focus on fairness, consumer protection and accountability Prior experience leading and developing teams, focused on enabling delivery through others and creating an inclusive, high-performing culture Prior experience engaging and influencing senior stakeholders, using clear written and verbal communication to build trusted relationships and explain complex issues effectively Essential: Legal or regulatory experience, ideally with knowledge of unfair terms legislation, including the Consumer Rights Act 2015 Advanced analytical skills and practical judgment, with the ability to weigh complex legal and regulatory risks Ability to balance strategic thinking with attention to detail and deliver high-quality outcomes Build effective relationships and collaborate with colleagues and stakeholders to meet shared objectives Familiarity with the FCA Handbook, particularly UNFCOG and the Consumer Duty A deep commitment to consumer protection and delivering positive outcomes for consumers Willingness to learn, curiosity and the ability to adjust in an evolving regulatory setting Benefits 28 days annual leave plus bank holidays Hybrid model where employees work a minimum of 40% in the office each month (expectation of 50% for senior leaders). Changing from September to a minimum of 50% in the office each month (expectation of 60% for Directors and Executive Directors) Non-contributory pension (8–12% depending on age) and life assurance at eight times your salary Private healthcare with Bupa, income protection and 24/7 Employee Assistance 35 hours of paid volunteering annually A flexible benefits scheme designed around your lifestyle For a full list of our benefits and our recruitment process as a whole visit our benefits page . Our values and culture Our colleagues are the key to our success as a regulator. We are committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive culture: one that’s free from discrimination and bias, celebrates difference and supports colleagues to deliver at their best. We believe that our differences and similarities enable us to be a better organisation – one that makes better decisions, drives innovation and delivers better regulation. If you require any adjustments due to a disability or condition, your recruiter is here to help - reach out for tailored support. We welcome diverse working styles and aim to find flexible solutions that suit both the role and individual needs, including options like part-time and job sharing where applicable. Disability confident: our hiring approach We’re proud to be a Disability Confident Employer and therefore, people or individuals with disabilities and long-term conditions who best meet the minimum criteria for a role will go through to the next stage of the recruitment process. In cases of high application volumes we may progress applicants whose experience most closely matches the role’s key requirements. Useful information and timelines Timeline: Job advert close: 4th August 2026 at Midnight CV Review/Shortlist: 5th and 6th August 2026 1st Stage Interviews: W/C 10th August 2026 Assessment: W/C 17th August 2026 2nd Stage Interviews : W/C 7th September 2026 Your Recruiter will discuss the process in detail with you during screening for the role, therefore, please make them aware if you are going to be unavailable for any date during this time.

item.title: Senior Data Engineer (7-Month Fixed-Term Contract)
item.description: Job title: Senior Data Engineer – 7-Month Fixed-Term Contract Division: Data, Technology and Innovation Department: Consumer Intelligence & Analytics Salary: National (Edinburgh and Leeds) ranging from £53,800 to £62,700 and London from £59,200 to £69,000 per annum (salary offered will be based on skills and experience) This role is graded as: Senior Associate - Regulatory Your external recruitment contact is Benjamin via benjamin.paulon@fca.org.uk Your internal recruitment contact is Pat via Patrycja.Matachowska@fca.org.uk Applications must be submitted through our online portal. Applications sent via social media or email will not be accepted. About the FCA and team We regulate financial services firms in the UK, to keep financial markets fair, thriving and effective. By joining us, you’ll play a key part in protecting consumers, driving economic growth, and shaping the future of UK finance services. The Data, Technology and Innovation (DTI) division enables the FCA to be a digital-first, data-led smart regulator by delivering a secure, agile, and cost-effective technology and data ecosystem that drives better decisions, transparency, and operational efficiency. Prior experience using data visualisation tools (e.g. Tableau). item.title: Senior Serious Organised Crime and Fraud Associate
item.description: Job Title: Senior Serious Organised Crime a nd Fraud Associate D ivision : Supervision, Policy & Competition (SPC) D epartment : Market Interventions Digital Assets (MIDA) Salary: National (Edinburgh and Leeds) ranging from £ 53,800 to £ 62 , 733 and London £ 59,200 to £ 6 9 ,000 (salary offered will be based on skills and experience) This role is graded as: Senior Associate – Regulatory Your external recruitment contact is Steve Christopher via steve.christopher@fca.org.uk . Your internal recruitment contact is Louis Vandenburgh via louis.vandenburgh@fca.org.uk . Applications must be submitted through our online portal. Applications sent via social media or email will not be accepted. About the FCA and team We regulate financial services firms in the UK, to keep financial markets fair, thriving and effective. By joining us, you’ll play a key part in protecting consumers, driving economic growth and shaping the future of financial services in the UK. SPC oversees firms and individuals (supervision), creates and reviews the rules by which they operate (policy) and identifies and remedies ineffective competition in markets (competition). The Serious Organised Crime (SOC) & Fraud Team, part of Market Interventions Digital Assets (MIDA), focuses on tackling the most serious criminal harms impacting the UK payments and digital assets sectors. We work at the intersection of intelligence, supervision, investigations and law enforcement partnerships, using the FCA’s regulatory tools to reduce harm and address criminal activity. It's an exciting time to join us. As the crypto and payments landscape continues to evolve, we’re expanding our capability to identify emerging threats, build on our intelligence-led approach, and deliver more impactful interventions across the system. To help us do that, we're recruiting two Senior Associates to join the team. We're looking for people who are passionate about tackling harm, curious about how criminal threats evolve, and willing to think creatively about how the FCA can identify and disrupt them. The two posts have different security-clearance requirements: one requires Developed Vetting (DV) and the other Security Clearance (SC). Candidates must hold, or be willing and eligible to obtain, the clearance required for the relevant post. Role responsibilities Lead a portfolio of complex, high-impact cases, developing and delivering strategies to reduce serious criminal harm through supervisory, regulatory and law-enforcement interventions, including, where appropriate , pioneering novel and precedent-setting approaches. Use intelligence, data and regulatory experience to identify emerging risks early, support innovative approaches to stop criminal activity and deliver meaningful outcomes across the financial system Build effective partnerships across the FCA, government and law enforcement agencies, contributing to significant investigations and developing expertise in financial crime, payments and digital assets regulation You will have the opportunity to make a meaningful impact by helping to prevent serious harm to UK consumers and society. You will work in a fast-paced and high-profile sector, build relationships across the FCA, government and law enforcement, and help shape how intelligence and data are translated into real-world outcomes. Skills required Minimum: For one role, hold, or be willing and eligible to obtain, Developed Vetting (DV) clearance. For the second role, hold, or be willing and eligible to obtain, Security Clearance (SC) Experience contributing to complex regulatory, intelligence or investigative work, using sound judgement and initiative to deliver effectively within multi-disciplinary teams. Experience of turning intelligence, insight or emerging-risk information into practical strategies, informed decisions and tangible outcomes through collaboration. Essential: Effective communication skills, with the ability to explain complex issues clearly and professionally, both in writing and verbally Demonstrated ability to manage competing priorities and deliver high-quality outcomes in a fast-paced and evolving environment Well-developed relationship-building skills, with the ability to collaborate and work effectively across a wide range of internal and external stakeholders Dedication to tackling financial crime and protecting consumers, with an investigative mindset and a drive to make a meaningful impact Benefits 25 days annual leave plus bank holidays Hybrid model where employees work a minimum of 40% in the office each month (expectation of 50% for senior leaders). Changing from September to a minimum of 50% in the office each month (expectation of 60% for Directors and Executive Directors) Non-contributory pension (8–12% depending on age) and life assurance at eight times your salary Private healthcare with Bupa, income protection and 24/7 Employee Assistance 35 hours of paid volunteering annually A flexible benefits scheme designed around your lifestyle For a full list of our benefits and our recruitment process as a whole visit our benefits page . Our values and culture Our colleagues are the key to our success as a regulator. We are committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive culture: one that’s free from discrimination and bias, celebrates difference and supports colleagues to deliver at their best. We believe that our differences and similarities enable us to be a better organisation – one that makes better decisions, drives innovation and delivers better regulation. If you require any adjustments due to a disability or condition, your recruiter is here to help - reach out for tailored support. We welcome diverse working styles and aim to find flexible solutions that suit both the role and individual needs, including options like part-time and job sharing where applicable. Disability confident: our hiring approach We’re proud to be a Disability Confident Employer and therefore, people or individuals with disabilities and long-term conditions who best meet the minimum criteria for a role will go through to the next stage of the recruitment process. In cases of high application volumes we may progress applicants whose experience most closely matches the role’s key requirements. Useful information and timelines Timeline: Job adverts close: 12th August 2026 at 23:59 CV Review/Shortlist: 14th August 2026 Interview s : 24th August to the 4th September 2026 Your Recruiter will discuss the process in detail with you during screening for the role, therefore, please make them aware if you are going to be unavailable for any date during this time For one role, DV clearance is required ( DV - Guidance ) - you will hold or will be required to obtain Developed Vetting (DV) level vetting For one role, SC clearance is required ( SC Guidance ) - you will hold or will be required to obtain Security Clearance (SC) level vetting

item.title: Senior Payments Supervisor
item.description: Job title: Senior Payments Supervisor Division: Supervision, Policy & Competition Department: Market Interventions – Payments Salary: National (Edinburgh and Leeds) ranging from £53,800 to £63,000 and London from £59,200 to £69,000 (salary offered will be based on skills and experience) This role is graded as: Senior Associate - Regulatory Your Internal recruitment contact is Louis Van Den Burg via louis.vandenburg@fca.org.uk Your external recruitment contact is Tahir Khan via tahir.khan1@fca.org.uk . Applications must be submitted through our online portal. Applications sent via social media or email will not be accepted. About the FCA and team We regulate financial services firms in the UK, to keep financial markets fair, thriving and effective. By joining us, you’ll play a key part in protecting consumers, driving economic growth, and shaping the future of UK finance services. SPC oversees firms and individuals (supervision), creates and reviews the rules by which they operate (policy) and identifies and remedies ineffective competition in markets (competition). Sitting in the Payments and Digital Assets directorate, the Market Intervention department supervises and drives regulatory standards in payments and crypto asset firms. The role of the payments services sector is rapidly becoming increasingly significant to the operation of the UK financial services system and beyond. This role presents an exciting opportunity for personal development and progression in this innovative, fast-expanding and technology-based sector. Role responsibilities: Getting to understand how regulated firms make their money, assessing the risks this presents to their customers and the markets in which they operate and determining how the firms manage these risks to stop customers being harmed, for example through the loss of their money Assessing regulated firms’ compliance against the relevant rules and requirements Working with senior industry figures to change how they operate if they are not meeting the rules and requirements and in the most severe cases taking regulatory action to stop firms operating their businesses Collaborating effectively and closely with colleagues across the FCA, other regulators and law enforcement agencies both in the UK and internationally Skills required: Minimum: Demonstrable experience of analysing complex information, and making sound, proportionate risk-based judgements and recommendations Demonstrable experience building and managing relationships with stakeholders at all levels, including senior management, showing effective written and verbal communication skills Experience managing a varied, high volume, and demanding workload, while maintaining well-developed organisational skills Essential: Good interpersonal and communication skills including ability to write briefings and letters, and capable of communicating effectively with a variety of internal and external stakeholders Experience of investigations or other problem-solving case work Curiosity, drive and motivation to continually learn about and investigate issues within the firms and industry we supervise Willingness to learn and develop yourself and others, taking on new responsibilities and adopting a flexible and pragmatic approach to your work Experience of effective teamwork, working with others to deliver outcomes and complete tasks Ability to prioritise workload and meet deadlines in a busy, demanding environment Ability to present yourself and the FCA in a professional and credible manner, with effective oral and presentation skills Benefits: 25 days annual leave plus bank holidays Hybrid model where employees work a minimum of 40% in the office each month (expectation of 50% for senior leaders). Changing from September to a minimum of 50% in the office each month (expectation of 60% for Directors and Executive Directors) Non-contributory pension (8–12% depending on age) and life assurance at eight times your salary Private healthcare with Bupa, income protection, and 24/7 Employee Assistance 35 hours of paid volunteering annually A flexible benefits scheme designed around your lifestyle For a full list of our benefits, and our recruitment process as a whole visit our benefits page . Our Values & Culture Our colleagues are the key to our success as a regulator. We are committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive culture: one that’s free from discrimination and bias, celebrates difference, and supports colleagues to deliver at their best. We believe that our differences and similarities enable us to be a better organisation – one that makes better decisions, drives innovation, and delivers better regulation. If you require any adjustments due to a disability or condition, your recruiter is here to help - reach out for tailored support. item.title: IT Sourcing Specialist
item.description: IT Sourcing Specialist Division: Operation Department: Procurement Salary: National (Edinburgh and Leeds) ranging from £39,100 to £42,000 and London from £41,600 to £45,000 (salary offered will be based on skills and experience) This role is graded as: Associate - Level 8 - Corporate Your external recruitment contact is Raimonda via raimonda.stankute@fca.org.uk . Your internal recruitment contact is Fizah via FizahFarouk.Ibrahim@fca.org.uk Applications must be submitted through our online portal. Applications sent via social media or email will not be accepted. About the FCA and team We regulate financial services firms in the UK, to keep financial markets fair, thriving and effective. By joining us, you’ll play a key part in protecting consumers, driving economic growth and shaping the future of UK finance services. Operations provides the platform and tools to enable the organisation to deliver on its statutory objectives. We work in partnership with the rest of the organisation to provide specialist support across a wide range of areas to deliver quality services and value for money. Procurement sits within the Finance and Corporate Services Directorate and works with departments across the FCA to source goods and services (£300m annual spend) required from third parties. We are responsible for ensuring procurements deliver contracts to agreed time, budget and quality, which are compliant with Public Procurement Regulations. Role responsibilities Deliver sourcing and contracting strategies across a portfolio of IT categories (including cloud services and end user computing) in line with the FCA procurement strategy Lead low risk/low complexity sourcing activity end-to-end (e.g., RFx, supplier negotiations, contract drafting/renewals), ensuring policy compliance, minimum standards and alignment to business needs Manage external supplier spend and drive value through strong commercial judgement, market awareness and effective sourcing/contracting approaches Ensure procurement projects meet objectives—delivered on time, within budget and to required quality standards, managing moderate complexity/risk renewals and sourcing projects Use and promote FCA procurement systems and processes, delivering work consistently with FCA procurement standards and governance Skills required Minimum: End-to-end procurement understanding: Able to scope business requirements, plan sourcing projects, produce tender documentation and follow appropriate project governance Experience running simple procurement exercises: Confident liaising with stakeholders, issuing tenders, evaluating responses and producing contract documents using templates Systems & development mindset: Working knowledge of procurement/purchasing systems and willingness to work towards a relevant qualification Essential: Clear, concise communication (written and verbal), able to explain complex issues succinctly to audiences at all levels Strong stakeholder management & influencing skills, building rapport with senior stakeholders and challenging constructively when needed Cross-functional collaboration, comfortable working with teams such as Legal, Finance, Risk, IT and other specialist functions Commercial & negotiation capability, applying market/industry insight to deliver tenders, negotiate contracts and drive value/innovation IT category awareness, ideally with knowledge of cloud services and end user computing (or ability to learn quickly) Sound judgement & business acumen, able to cut through complexity, make timely decisions and keep focus on the bigger picture Organisational resilience & delivery focus, able to prioritise/multitask under pressure with a methodical, detail-oriented approach and strong customer service mindset Benefits 25 days annual leave plus bank holidays Non-contributory pension (8–12% depending on age) and life assurance at eight times your salary Private healthcare with Bupa, income protection and 24/7 Employee Assistance 35 hours of paid volunteering annually Hybrid model where employees work a minimum of 40% in the office each month (expectation of 50% for senior leaders). Changing from September to a minimum of 50% in the office each month (expectation of 60% for Directors and Executive Directors) A flexible benefits scheme designed around your lifestyle For a full list of our benefits and our recruitment process as a whole visit our benefits page . Our values and culture Our colleagues are the key to our success as a regulator. We are committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive culture: one that’s free from discrimination and bias, celebrates difference and supports colleagues to deliver at their best. We believe that our differences and similarities enable us to be a better organisation – one that makes better decisions, drives innovation and delivers better regulation. If you require any adjustments due to a disability or condition, your recruiter is here to help - reach out for tailored support. We welcome diverse working styles and aim to find flexible solutions that suit both the role and individual needs, including options like part-time and job sharing where applicable. Disability confident: our hiring approach We’re proud to be a Disability Confident Employer and therefore, people or individuals with disabilities and long-term conditions who best meet the minimum criteria for a role will go through to the next stage of the recruitment process. In cases of high application volumes we may progress applicants whose experience most closely matches the role’s key requirements. Useful information and timelines Timeline: Job advert closes: Midnight, 20th May 2026 CV Review/Shortlist: 22nd May 2026 Interview: w/c 1st June 2026 Your Recruiter will discuss the process in detail with you during screening for the role, therefore, please make them aware if you are going to be unavailable for any date during this time.

item.title: Senior Fraud Policy Associate
item.description: Job title: Senior Fraud Policy Associate Division: Supervision, Policy and Competition Department: Office for Professional Body Anti-Money Laundering Supervision & Fraud & Financial Crime Salary: National (Edinburgh and Leeds) ranging from £53,800 to £68,000 and London £59,200 to £75,000 per annum (Salary offered will be based on skills and experience) This role is graded as: Senior Associate, Regulatory Your external recruitment contact is Shafika via Shafika.shaikh@fca.org.uk Your internal recruitment contact is Isabelle via Isabelle.groves@fca.org.uk Applications must be submitted through our online portal. Applications sent via social media or email will not be accepted. About the FCA and team We regulate financial services firms in the UK, to keep financial markets fair, thriving and effective. By joining us, you’ll play a key part in protecting consumers, driving economic growth, and shaping the future of UK finance services. SPC oversees firms and individuals (supervision), creates and reviews the rules by which they operate (policy) and identifies and remedies ineffective competition in markets (competition). Sitting in Specialist Directorate, this role is supporting the organisation’s lead specialist on fraud policy. You will help shape and deliver high-profile, technically complex fraud policy workstreams, provide expert advice across the organisation, and influence external partners to drive joined-up, cross-system approaches to preventing fraud and combating financial crime. Role responsibilities Lead and deliver fraud policy workstreams, undertaking research, analysing regulatory developments, and producing evidence-based policy recommendations to inform decision-making Provide specialist fraud policy expertise by delivering technical advice, policy analysis, briefings, and guidance to colleagues across the organisation Support the development of fraud policy, assessing emerging fraud risks, regulatory changes, and market trends to help shape the organisation's strategic approach Coordinate stakeholder engagement activities, working with government bodies, industry representatives, and regulatory partners to support fraud-related initiatives and policy outcomes Build and maintain effective working relationships with internal and external stakeholders, fostering collaboration and ensuring a coordinated approach to policy development and implementation Contribute to team capability and knowledge sharing, supporting colleagues through collaboration, mentoring, and the dissemination of best practice and policy insights Skills required Minimum: Prior experience of drafting or shaping policy Prior experience of financial crime and financial crime landscape Demonstrable stakeholder engagement skills Essential: Experience of leading projects and supporting others Solid analytical skills, including ability to analyse complex quantitative and qualitative data, draw reasoned and logical inferences, and reach robust and practical conclusions within tight deadlines Interest in policymaking and developing regulation that protects client assets and reduces harm in firm failures Collaborative colleague who works inclusively and shares knowledge Experience of delivering a variety of competing high-profile tasks with challenging targets under tight deadlines, prioritising appropriately and escalating where necessary An ability to identify and manage key stakeholders, build rapport and constructive relationships, and be a credible representative for the FCA Understanding of the financial services market in the UK and how firms operate within it Benefits 25 days annual leave plus bank holidays Non-contributory pension (8–12% depending on age) and life assurance at eight times your salary Private healthcare with Bupa, income protection, and 24/7 Employee Assistance 35 hours of paid volunteering annually Hybrid model where employees work a minimum of 40% in the office each month (expectation of 50% for senior leaders). Changing from September to a minimum of 50% in the office each month (expectation of 60% for Directors and Executive Directors) A flexible benefits scheme designed around your lifestyle For a full list of our benefits, and our recruitment process as a whole visit our benefits page . Our values and culture Our colleagues are the key to our success as a regulator. We are committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive culture: one that’s free from discrimination and bias, celebrates difference, and supports colleagues to deliver at their best. We believe that our differences and similarities enable us to be a better organisation – one that makes better decisions, drives innovation, and delivers better regulation. If you require any adjustments due to a disability or condition, your recruiter is here to help - reach out for tailored support. We welcome diverse working styles and aim to find flexible solutions that suit both the role and individual needs, including options like part-time and job sharing where applicable. Disability confident: our hiring approach We’re proud to be a Disability Confident Employer , and therefore, people or individuals with disabilities and long-term conditions who best meet the minimum criteria for a role will go through to the next stage of the recruitment process. In cases of high application volumes we may progress applicants whose experience most closely matches the role’s key requirements. Useful information and timelines Advert Close Date: 3rd August 2026 CV Review/Shortlist: 6th August 2026 First Interview: W/C 17th August 2026 Your Recruiter will discuss the process in detail with you during screening for the role, therefore, please make them aware if you are going to be unavailable for any date during this time. SC Clearance is required for this role ( SC Guidance ) - you will hold or will be required to obtain Security Check (SC) level vetting
